project: SessionForge Agent — Session Output Debug Loop
description: Fix zero session output: replace non-overlapped CreatePipe with exec.Cmd/os.Pipe (overlapped handles required by libuv/node.js), validate output flows end-to-end, add automated regression guard.

userStories.title: Verify spawnWithPipes uses exec.Cmd (overlapped pipes)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to confirm the current spawnWithPipes implementation uses exec.Cmd + os.Pipe instead of windows.CreatePipe, so libuv/node.js can write to stdout.
acceptanceCriteria: agent/internal/session/pty_windows.go spawnWithPipes uses exec.Cmd, not windows.CreatePipe
go build ./... passes with no errors
go vet ./... passes with no errors
userStories.notes: exec.Cmd/os.Pipe creates FILE_FLAG_OVERLAPPED handles. windows.CreatePipe creates non-overlapped handles that libuv silently fails to write to. Root cause confirmed by spawn-diag.exe (0 bytes) vs pipedump.exe (output flows).

userStories.title: Build sessionforge.exe and confirm binary is newer than service binary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a fresh sessionforge.exe built from the fixed source so the service can be updated.
acceptanceCriteria: go build -o sessionforge-new.exe ./cmd/sessionforge/ completes with exit code 0
sessionforge-new.exe file timestamp is newer than C:\Users\Jakeb\AppData\Local\Programs\sessionforge\sessionforge.exe
userStories.notes: Build must succeed before service swap. Check timestamp to confirm we are not running a stale binary.

userStories.title: Create spawn smoke test: node.exe produces output via exec.Cmd
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an automated test that spawns node.exe via exec.Cmd and asserts stdout bytes arrive, so we can catch pipe regressions before deploying.
acceptanceCriteria: Test asserts output contains PING within 10 seconds
Test has build tag: //go:build windows && integration
userStories.notes: This is the regression guard. If someone reverts to windows.CreatePipe, this test fails immediately.

userStories.title: Create diagnostic script: scripts/check-session-output.ps1
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a PowerShell script that checks whether the service is producing session output, reports the spawn tier selected, and prints a pass/fail verdict.
acceptanceCriteria: Script reads last 100 lines of C:\Users\Jakeb\.sessionforge\agent.log
Script reports: service account (LocalSystem or user), spawn tier selected (wsl/gitbash/conpty/pipes), whether session_output chunks appear in log
Script prints PASS if session_output chunks found, FAIL with diagnosis if not
Script can be run as: powershell scripts/check-session-output.ps1
userStories.notes: Quick human-runnable check after service restart. Replaces manual log grepping.

userStories.title: Auto-populate claude_config_dir in config.toml at service install
userStories.description: As a developer, I need service install to automatically detect and write claude_config_dir to config.toml so CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR is always injected into spawned sessions without manual editing.
acceptanceCriteria: If ~/.claude exists, cfg.ClaudeConfigDir is set before saving config
If config already has a non-empty ClaudeConfigDir, it is not overwritten
userStories.notes: Currently requires manual config.toml edit. Without CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR, Claude returns 'Not logged in' when service runs as LocalSystem.

userStories.title: Update tasks/lessons.md with exec.Cmd fix (L008)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the lessons file updated with the confirmed fix so future engineers know why exec.Cmd is used instead of CreateProcess.
acceptanceCriteria: L008 documents: spawnWithPipes rewritten to use exec.Cmd + os.Pipe
L008 references L001 (overlapped pipe root cause)
L008 states the acceptance test: TestSpawnWithPipes_NodeOutputFlows
userStories.notes: Self-improvement loop: every fix gets documented so it cannot regress silently.
